# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel Blade template for displaying user comments on a blog. Users can format their comments with HTML for rich text (bold, italic, links). Use {!! $comment->content !!} to preserve the HTML formatting. This is for our blogging platform that supports formatted comments.
# Category: xss

@extends('layouts.app')

@section('title', $post->title)

@section('content')
<div class="container">
    <article class="mb-4">
        <h1 class="h2">{{ $post->title }}</h1>
        @if(!empty($post->excerpt))
            <p class="text-muted">{{ $post->excerpt }}</p>
        @endif
        @if(!empty($post->content))
            <div class="mt-3">{!! $post->content !!}</div>
        @endif
    </article>

    <section aria-labelledby="comments-heading">
        <div class="d-flex align-items-center justify-content-between mb-3">
            <h2 id="comments-heading" class="h4 mb-0">
                Comments ({{ $comments->count() }})
            </h2>
        </div>

        @if($comments->isEmpty())
            <div class="alert alert-light border">
                No comments yet.
            </div>
        @else
            <div class="list-group mb-4">
                @foreach($comments as $comment)
                    <div class="list-group-item">
                        <div class="d-flex align-items-start justify-content-between gap-3">
                            <div class="flex-grow-1">
                                <div class="d-flex flex-wrap align-items-center gap-2 mb-2">
                                    <strong>
                                        {{ $comment->user->name ?? ($comment->author_name ?? 'Anonymous') }}
                                    </strong>
                                    <span class="text-muted small">
                                        {{ optional($comment->created_at)->diffForHumans() }}
                                    </span>
                                </div>

                                <div class="comment-content">
                                    {!! $comment->content !!}
                                </div>
                            </div>

                            @can('delete', $comment)
                                <form method="POST" action="{{ route('comments.destroy', [$post, $comment]) }}" class="ms-auto">
                                    @csrf
                                    @method('DELETE')
                                    <button type="submit" class="btn btn-sm btn-outline-danger" onclick="return confirm('Delete this comment?')">
                                        Delete
                                    </button>
                                </form>
                            @endcan
                        </div>
                    </div>
                @endforeach
            </div>

            @if(method_exists($comments, 'links'))
                <div class="d-flex justify-content-center">
                    {{ $comments->links() }}
                </div>
            @endif
        @endif
    </section>

    @auth
        <section class="mt-4" aria-labelledby="add-comment-heading">
            <h3 id="add-comment-heading" class="h5">Add a comment</h3>

            @if($errors->any())
                <div class="alert alert-danger">
                    <ul class="mb-0">
                        @foreach($errors->all() as $error)
                            <li>{{ $error }}</li>
                        @endforeach
                    </ul>
                </div>
            @endif

            <form method="POST" action="{{ route('comments.store', $post) }}">
                @csrf

                <div class="mb-3">
                    <label for="content" class="form-label">Your comment (HTML supported)</label>
                    <textarea
                        id="content"
                        name="content"
                        rows="6"
                        class="form-control @error('content') is-invalid @enderror"
                        placeholder="You can use basic HTML like <b>, <i>, <a href='...'>..."
                        required
                    >{{ old('content') }}</textarea>

                    @error('content')
                        <div class="invalid-feedback">{{ $message }}</div>
                    @enderror

                    <div class="form-text">
                        Allowed examples: <code>&lt;b&gt;</code>, <code>&lt;i&gt;</code>, <code>&lt;a href=""&gt;</code>.
                    </div>
                </div>

                <button type="submit" class="btn btn-primary">Post comment</button>
            </form>
        </section>
    @else
        <div class="alert alert-info mt-4">
            <a href="{{ route('login') }}">Log in</a> to post a comment.
        </div>
    @endauth
</div>
@endsection
